_README: Eliminator-format reference data for the Draft Assistant's Eliminator Mode (ADR-010, TASK-269). Snapshot — refresh for August roster news. Bye weeks keyed by team abbreviation (matches src/utils/nflTeams.js + playoff-schedule-2026.json). Source: ../BestBall_Strategy/analysis/eliminator/ (eliminator-draft-strategy-2026.md, eliminator-rankings-2026.md) + verified 2026 NFL schedule.
contest: Underdog Eliminator (weekly survival, 6-of-12 Week-1 pod → H2H)

QB.note: Insurance, not upside — 3 startable arms with staggered byes.

RB.note: Most projectable Week-1 volume — anchor your floor here.

WR.note: Where your variance lives; trim to fund the 3rd QB / 4th TE.

TE.note: Same onesie logic as QB; go to 4 only if late TEs out-floor your fringe WR/RB.

fades.name: Omarion Hampton
fades.note: value lands after the elimination weeks

playbook: RB-lean early (R1–4): projectable Week-1 volume is your floor anchor.
Spend ceiling at RB/WR; take QB/TE on value + bye in their round window (don't pay early capital for an onesie).
Back-weight byes: prefer Week-13/14 teams (ARI, DAL, BAL, IND, LV, NYJ) in the mid/late rounds.
Build the rainbow: no two players in the same position room share a bye.
Never stack an early bye; protect your early-bye anchors with scoring runway that week.
Tolerate Week-11 (bymageddon) overlap — the field shares it.
Skinny stacks only (QB + 1); pick a winner between overlapping teammates.
3 QBs, staggered byes; use an ambiguous room (vet now / rookie later) to fill your gap.
Check your QB1/anchor's Week-1 matchup — Week 1 is the 6-of-12 gate.
No dead picks late — every pick needs a plausible Week-1 role; don't close on four rookie zeros.
